description: Ground-truth anomaly events used for model evaluation. Each date represents a known market crash or extreme event. Data range: 2010-01-01 to present. Note: Lehman Brothers (2008-09-15) is excluded as it predates the data range.
coverage_note: Events span full data range 2010-2026 with no gaps > 18 months

impact_scale.extreme: Cross-market systemic event, >30% drawdown or VIX > 50
impact_scale.high: Major multi-market selloff, VIX spike > 30%, significant drawdown
impact_scale.medium: Elevated volatility, sector or asset-class specific impact

events.description: Market dropped 9% in minutes then recovered same day. Algo-driven cascade failure.
S&P downgraded US credit rating from AAA to AA+. Dow dropped 634 pts in one session.
Global markets crashed on China economic slowdown fears. Shanghai Composite dropped 8.5%.
VIX spiked 115% overnight. Short-volatility ETPs collapsed. Dow fell 1,175 pts — largest single-day point drop at the time.
S&P 500 fell nearly 20% from peak. Fed rate-hike fears, US government shutdown, trade war concerns.
First major crash day of COVID panic. S&P 500 fell 3.4% as virus spread outside China became clear. Start of the fastest bear market in history.
S&P 500 dropped 34% in 5 weeks. VIX reached all-time high of 82.69. Circuit breakers triggered multiple times.
Reddit WallStreetBets-driven short squeeze sent GME up 2,500% in days. Robinhood halted trading. Hedge funds lost billions.
Markets dropped sharply on aggressive Fed rate-hike signals. Nasdaq fell 15% in January. Growth stocks hardest hit.
$40 billion in crypto wiped out in 72 hours. UST stablecoin de-pegged. Contagion spread across entire crypto market.
UK bond market meltdown after Kwarteng mini-budget. Pound hit all-time low vs USD. Bank of England emergency intervention.
SVB failed in 48 hours — largest US bank failure since 2008. Signature Bank also collapsed. Systemic contagion fears triggered emergency Fed backstop.
Bank of Japan rate hike triggered global carry trade unwind. VIX spiked to 65 — highest since COVID. Nikkei fell 12% in one day. Nasdaq dropped 6%.
